Savings & Energy Choices

  • Home energy choices
  • Business energy choices
  • Renewable energy
  • Electric vehicles
  • Customer generation
  • Time of Use

Beat the heat and keep costs down

As temperatures rise, so can your energy use. We're here to help, with energy choices and discounts that save you money and tools to keep your home cool and comfortable all summer long.

En español

Simple ways to keep your home cool and save

78º is great

Set your thermostat at 78º F and use a smart or programmable thermostat to automatically adjust the temperature.

Keep cool air in

Keep blinds and curtains closed during the day. Open windows during cooler evening hours.

It pays to be a fan

Use ceiling, attic and portable fans to stay cool. Remember to turn them off when you leave the room. 

Clean and tune

Have your central air conditioner tuned up and clean or replace filters monthly for efficient operation.

Wait 'til late

Run the dryer and dishwasher at night on hot days and let your dishes air dry. 

Wash in cold

Wash clothes in cold water and clean the lint filter in the dryer after every use.

Low energy cooking

Minimize indoor heat by using a microwave, toaster oven or outdoor grill instead of the oven.

Unplug

Unplug appliances and electronics when not in use. To make it easier, plug home electronics and computers into smart power strips.

Beat the heat

If you have a pool, use an efficient, variable-speed pool pump to save on energy costs. Also be sure to keep the intake grates clear of debris.

Plant trees

Consider landscaping with leafy shade trees. Plant them on the southeast and southwest sides of your home.

Earn rebates and discounts on home upgrades

    1. Oregon
    2. Washington
    3. California
    1. SAVINGS AND REBATES
    2. MORE ENERGY CHOICES

    Cooling solutions

    Energy Trust of Oregon can help you save on equipment that improves comfort and lowers energy costs. 

    Learn More

    Cool Keeper

    Customers in select areas can sign up and help ease demand for energy during select high-demand periods. Participants earn $30 annually.

    Find out more

    Solar incentives

    Energy Trust of Oregon offers incentives for solar installations, with increased rebates for income-qualified households.

    Get details

    Wattsmart Batteries

    If you have onsite solar generation, you can earn incentives for allowing us to automatically manage your battery as part of our grid.

    Wattsmart Battery

    Track usage

    See your monthly, daily and hourly energy use to identify ways to save more.

    Time of Use

    Time of Use plan participants can earn lower prices by shifting energy use to off-peak hours when the demand for electricity is lower.
